Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops skills relevant for employment and further scientific study (Target 4.4).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides foundational skills in probability, a tool for innovation and technological upgrading in high-value sectors, contributing to economic productivity (Target 8.2).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course teaches fundamental probability theory, a cornerstone for enhancing scientific research, technological capabilities, and innovation across various industrial sectors (Target 9.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Builds foundational knowledge for statistics, essential for statistical capacity-building and developing measurements of progress for sustainable development (Targets 17.18, 17.19).
